summary: Backend testing of NEW AI image generation (Gemini Nano Banana) endpoints + admin product filter additions, plus full regression. All 37/37 tests passed (11 new + 26 regression). Real Gemini gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview calls executed: single generation produced a valid JPEG (>1KB, correct magic bytes) accessible via /api/files/, bulk with limit=2 successfully processed 2 of 825 unimaged products in ~26s. Auth gating verified for anonymous and non-admin users (403). search and missing_images query params on /api/admin/products work correctly.

minor.issue: When Gemini returns no images, helper returns None and endpoint raises 500 'Gagal generate gambar'. A 502/503 would more accurately reflect upstream LLM failure vs server bug. Cosmetic.
Calls run serially (~25s each) inside the request. With limit=10 the request can take ~4 minutes and may hit ingress timeouts. Consider background task / streaming progress.
Still return 503 because RAJAONGKIR_API_KEY is empty - documented as expected (carried over from iteration_3).

action_items: OPTIONAL: Refactor bulk image generation to a background job (e.g., asyncio task / job queue) so the HTTP request doesn't block for up to ~4 minutes on limit=10.
OPTIONAL: When Gemini returns no images, propagate a 502/503 upstream-failure status code instead of a generic 500 so the admin UI can distinguish LLM outages from internal errors.
OPTIONAL (carryover): Set RAJAONGKIR_API_KEY in /app/backend/.env to enable shipping endpoints.
OPTIONAL (carryover): server.py is now ~1160 lines - modularize into APIRouter files.
critical_code_review_comments: _generate_product_image swallows ALL exceptions and returns None, masking real errors (network, auth, parsing). Log the exception type and include it in the response or sentry. Currently the admin sees only generic 'Gagal generate gambar' 500s.

Bulk endpoint executes Gemini calls sequentially inside the request handler. Each call ~25s -> with default limit=5 the request may take ~2 min. Ingress timeouts (commonly 60s) could kill the connection before MongoDB updates land, though writes themselves are atomic per product. Move to background task with progress polling.

Both new endpoints call put_object (Emergent storage) but the helper does not wrap put_object in its own try; a storage failure currently surfaces as None -> 500, indistinguishable from LLM failure. Differentiate the two.

/api/admin/products `missing_images` ignores products where 'images' is an array containing only empty strings. Edge case unlikely from Shopee import but worth a defensive filter ('images.0': {'$in': [None, '']}).

Single-image endpoint replaces images[0] with the new AI image while preserving the rest of the array (good). Bulk endpoint OVERWRITES the entire images array unconditionally (line 874). This is fine today (only runs for products with empty images) but is fragile if the filter ever changes. Consider mirroring single-endpoint logic.
server.py is now 1160 lines (exceeds 700-line guideline). Split into routers/ modules.
Carryover: GET /api/products/{id} still returns inactive products (no is_active filter).
test_credentials: Admin panel: username=Admin password=Kodok5561 (POST /api/auth/admin-login). Email fallback: admin@almiraflorist.com / Kodok5561.
seed_data_creation: Test file creates TEST_AI_CAT_* category and 3 TEST_AI_Monstera_Deliciosa_* products with empty images (module-scoped, cleaned up at module teardown). Two of those (or other unimaged DB products) end up with generated AI images after the bulk test - those images are real Emergent storage objects under almira/ai-images/{product_id}.{png|jpg} and are NOT deleted by the test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend has 825 products without images at time of test (823 mentioned + 2 created during test cleanup). Two new test files: test_ai_image_generation.py (11 tests, includes 3 real Gemini calls ~75s total) and test_almira_backend.py (26 tests, ~7s). Reuse them; bulk test pulls real products from the DB and writes real images. If running repeatedly, expect 'remaining' count to drop. AI gen costs real credits - don't run TestSingleImageGeneration / TestBulkImageGeneration in a tight loop. Auth is cookie-based on requests.Session, both endpoints correctly enforce admin role.
rca of the issue: No bugs. Generated images verified: response gives {image_path, image_url}; GET /api/files/{path} returns image/* content-type with JPEG magic bytes 0xFFD8FF and >1KB payload; product.images[0] is set to the same path. Bulk returns processed/success/failed/remaining/remaining_before/results[] and remaining decreases (or stays equal on failure) after the run.
